title: Preset themes
lead: Three named brand presets, each one swapping the entire token set with a single attribute on <html>.
body:
At a glance

PresetActivateFeel
Default(none)Tabler-ish blue with neutral palette. Good baseline for new apps.
Lineardata-cms-theme="linear"Dark by default. Violet accent (#5e6ad2). Inter type. Hairline borders, 6 px radii. Tight controls.
Tablerdata-cms-theme="tabler"Light. Tabler blue (#206bc4). 4 px radii. Defined elevation tiers. Light + dark variants.
Materialdata-cms-theme="material"Material You. Tonal-purple (#6750a4). 12 px radii. Roboto. Larger 40 px controls. Light + dark.

Applying a preset

Set the attribute on <html> in your shell. The cleanest place is from app.js:

document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-cms-theme', 'linear');
document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-cms-density', 'compact');

For cms-vercel, hand it through the runtime themeMode option (light/dark only) and inject the preset attribute via your shell template. Custom shells set the attribute directly.

Combining with dark mode

data-cms-theme and data-theme are independent. Combine them freely:

<html data-cms-theme="material" data-theme="dark">

Linear is dark-only by default — the preset overrides every neutral with the Linear palette regardless of data-theme. Tabler and Material define both light and dark variants.

Building a custom preset

Override every neutral inside a single attribute selector and ship it as part of your app's CSS:

:root[data-cms-theme="acme"] {
  --cms-primary:        #ff6f3c;
  --cms-primary-hover:  #d75a2e;
  --cms-primary-active: #b04a26;
  --cms-primary-fade:   rgb(255 111 60 / 0.12);

  --cms-bg:        #fbf7f2;
  --cms-surface:   #ffffff;
  --cms-surface-2: #f4ede4;
  --cms-border:    #e6dccd;
  --cms-text:      #1d1813;
  --cms-text-muted:#6f5a47;

  --cms-radius: 10px;
  --cms-font:   "Söhne", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif;
}

Then activate with <html data-cms-theme="acme">. See Design tokens for the full variable list.

title: Design tokens
lead: Every visual decision in prism is a CSS variable. Override one in your app's CSS and every component picks it up — no recompile, no fork.
body:
Palette

VariableDefault (light)Used by
--cms-primary#2563ebbtn-primary, navbar bg, focus rings, active nav, sort indicator…
--cms-primary-hover#1d4ed8btn-primary :hover
--cms-primary-active#1e40afbtn-primary :active
--cms-primary-fadergb(37 99 235 / .08)row hover, badge bg, soft button bg…
--cms-success / --cms-success-fade#16a34abtn-success, badge-success, alert-success
--cms-warning / --cms-warning-fade#f59e0bbtn-warning, badge-warning, alert-warning
--cms-danger / --cms-danger-fade#dc2626btn-danger, badge-danger, alert-danger
--cms-info / --cms-info-fade#0891b2btn-info, badge-info, alert-info

Neutrals

VariableDefault (light)Used by
--cms-bg#f8fafcpage background
--cms-surface#ffffffcards, tables, modals, navbar
--cms-surface-2#f1f5f9striped rows, table heads, kbd, palette panel
--cms-surface-hover#f8fafchover backgrounds in lists
--cms-border#e2e8f0card / table borders
--cms-border-strong#cbd5e1input borders, scrollbar thumb
--cms-text#0f172abody text, headings
--cms-text-muted#64748bcaptions, table head text
--cms-text-faint#94a3b8placeholders, sort indicators

Geometry, type, motion

VariableDefault
--cms-radius / --cms-radius-sm / --cms-radius-lg8px / 4px / 14px
--cms-control-h / --cms-control-h-sm / --cms-control-h-lg36px / 28px / 44px
--cms-fontsystem stack
--cms-font-monosystem mono stack
--cms-text-xs / -sm / -md / -lg0.75 / 0.875 / 1 / 1.125rem
--cms-shadow-xs / -sm / / -lgthree elevation tiers
--cms-shadow-focusfocus-ring shadow
--cms-dur / --cms-dur-slow160ms / 280ms (collapses to 0 under prefers-reduced-motion)
--cms-ease / --cms-ease-in / --cms-ease-outcubic-bezier curves

Dark mode

Set data-theme="dark" on <html>. prism redefines every token under the dark selector — no further work needed.

<html data-theme="dark">

Auto mode that follows the OS setting:

<html data-theme="auto">

title: Variants & density
lead: Modifier classes you call from .cms via existing qualifier props. Density is a page-level preset that re-tones every control.
body:
Calling variants from .cms

The CaseMaster runtimes already accept class strings through standard qualifiers — no new qualifier system needed:

<@page/data/table iterator: [items], group: 'list',
                  tableClass: 'cms-table-elevated cms-table-compact'>

<@page/button class: 'btn btn-soft-primary btn-pill', label: 'Approve'>

<@page/alert  class: 'alert alert-warning cms-alert-subtle',
              text:  'Two items below safety stock.'>

Table variants

  • cms-table-compact — dense list view, smaller font.
  • cms-table-comfortable — generous vertical breathing room.
  • cms-table-elevated — higher shadow + larger radii; great when the table sits on a card-less surface.
  • cms-table-flush — no outer border or shadow; use inside an existing card body.
  • cms-table-hover-only — drops zebra stripes; only the hover row highlights.

Button variants

  • btn-soft-{primary,success,danger,warning,info} — tinted background, full-saturation text. Linear / Stripe style.
  • btn-ghost-{primary,success,danger} — text-only until hover. Great for icon bars.
  • btn-pill — fully rounded.
  • btn-square — sharp corners.
  • btn-icon-only — square aspect ratio. Pair with aria-label.

Card variants

  • cms-card-elevated — bigger shadow, no border.
  • cms-card-outlined — strong border, no shadow.
  • cms-card-status-{primary,success,warning,danger,info} — coloured left strip.
  • cms-card-interactive — hover-lift; for link cards.
  • cms-stat-card — KPI tile shape (label + value + trend).

Alert variants

  • cms-alert-subtle — minimal background, just a coloured left bar.
  • cms-alert-soft — tinted background, no border, larger radius.

Input variants

  • cms-input-filled — coloured surface, only bottom border on focus.
  • cms-input-underlined — Material-style, just a bottom rule.

Density preset

Page-level — shrinks or grows every control system-wide:

<html data-cms-density="compact">       <!-- back-office dashboards -->
<html data-cms-density="comfortable">   <!-- larger touch targets -->

title: Configuration reference
lead: Disable any enhancer at runtime via window.cmsUi. Disable a single element with data-cms-no-enhance.
body:
Global config

<script>
  window.cmsUi = {
    tables:     { search: true, sort: true, csvExport: false },
    forms:      { tomSelect: true, flatpickr: true, selectThreshold: 8 },
    shortcuts:  true,
    command:    true,
    inlineEdit: false,
    confirm:    true,
    loading:    true,
    toasts:     true,
    modalLinks: true,
  };
</script>
<script type="module" src="/static/lib/prism/prism.js"></script>

Reference

KeyDefaultEffect when off
tablesonNo sortable headers, no toolbar.
tables.searchonToolbar shown, but no filter input.
tables.sortonHeaders no longer clickable to sort.
tables.csvExportonNo CSV button.
tables.columnToggleonNo Columns button.
tables.minRowsToEnhance2Tables with fewer rows are skipped (no toolbar).
formsonBare <select> / <input type=date>.
forms.tomSelectonSelects stay native.
forms.flatpickronDate inputs stay native.
forms.selectThreshold8Selects with fewer options stay native unless tagged data-cms-search.
confirmondata-cms-confirm ignored.
loadingonNo top progress bar.
toastsoncms-toast events ignored; X-Cms-Toast header ignored.
shortcutson/, ?, g X chords inert.
commandonCmd+K palette disabled.
modalLinksondata-cms-modal-link falls back to navigation.
inlineEditondata-cms-inline-edit cells become static.

Per-element opt-out

Skip a single element with data-cms-no-enhance:

<table class='table table-striped' data-cms-no-enhance>...</table>

<select class='form-control' data-cms-no-enhance>...</select>

<a href='/delete/42' data-cms-no-enhance>Skip enhancer</a>

Skip an entire subtree by adding the attribute on a wrapper:

<div data-cms-no-enhance>
  <!-- nothing inside this is enhanced -->
</div>

Body-level attributes

AttributeSet onPurpose
data-cms-theme<html>Apply a brand preset (linear / tabler / material / custom).
data-cms-density<html>Compact or comfortable controls.
data-theme<html>light / dark / auto.
dir<html>RTL flips toolbar / sidebar / dropdowns.
data-cms-runtime<body> (set by prism)Diagnostic — shows detected runtime (dotnet / vercel / unknown).
data-cms-ui-version<body> (set by prism)Bundle version, e.g. 0.3.0.

title: .NET runtime integration
lead: Vendoring or CDN, app.css / app.js hooks, dark mode, RTL, Select2 coexistence.
body:
The .NET runtime ships an HTML shell that always loads static/css/app.css and static/js/app.js at the bottom of every page. App authors hook in through those two files; we never patch the framework.

Recommended: vendored static files

Survives git pull of the framework. Works offline. Pinned versions.

1. Drop the bundle

# PowerShell — adjust the runtime root path for your install
$src  = "$env:USERPROFILE\.prism-src\node_modules\casemaster-prism\dist"
$dst  = "C:\Casemaster-WMS\runtime\static\lib\prism"
Remove-Item $dst -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
New-Item   $dst -ItemType Directory | Out-Null
Copy-Item  "$src\*" $dst -Recurse

Or vendor the files directly into your app repo once and check them in — they're stable across patch versions.

2. static/css/app.css

/* App-wide overrides above; everything below is owned by prism. */
@import url('/static/lib/prism/prism.min.css');

/* Per-app brand override */
:root { --cms-primary: #0ea5e9; }

3. static/js/app.js

(function () {
  window.cmsUi = window.cmsUi || {};

  var s = document.createElement('script');
  s.type = 'module';
  s.src = '/static/lib/prism/prism.js';
  document.body.appendChild(s);
})();

Alternative: CDN reference

Simpler for prototypes; needs outbound HTTPS at request time.

/* app.css */
@import url('https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/casemaster-prism@1/dist/prism.min.css');
// app.js
const s = document.createElement('script');
s.type = 'module';
s.src = 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/casemaster-prism@1/dist/prism.js';
document.body.appendChild(s);

Apply a preset theme

The shell is owned by the framework. Set the attribute from app.js:

document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-cms-theme', 'tabler');
document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-cms-density', 'comfortable');

Coexistence with Select2

The .NET runtime loads scripts in this order:

jquery.min.js
select2.min.js
bootstrap.bundle.min.js
cm.js
app.js                ← prism hooks in here

By the time prism runs, Select2 has already wrapped its targets and added .select2-hidden-accessible to the underlying <select>. Our forms enhancer skips those — Select2's UX wins. No cm.js collisions because we use data-cms-enhanced flags to mark our work.

title: cms-vercel integration
lead: One option in api/index.ts. Granular flags. Vendoring. Custom shells.
body:
Basic setup

// api/index.ts
import { createHandler } from 'cms-vercel';

export default createHandler({
  ui: 'pro', // theme + enhancers
});

The runtime injects the <link> / <script> tags into the default HTML shell.

Shorthand values

ValueEffect
'pro'Theme + enhancers (recommended).
'theme-only'CSS only.
'enhancers-only'JS only.
falseOff (default).

Granular options

createHandler({
  ui: {
    theme:       true,
    themeAccent: '#7c3aed',
    themeMode:   'auto',                    // 'auto' | 'light' | 'dark'
    enhancers:   { tables: true, shortcuts: false },
    version:     '1',                       // pin a CDN version
    scriptUrl:   '/static/lib/prism/prism.js', // override JS bundle URL
  },
});

Vendoring

If you can't reach jsDelivr from production, vendor the files into your public/ directory and pass the URLs explicitly:

createHandler({
  ui: {
    theme:     '/static/lib/prism/prism.min.css',
    scriptUrl: '/static/lib/prism/prism.js',
    enhancers: true,
  },
});

Drop dist/prism.min.css, dist/prism.js, and the dist/chunks/ folder into your repo's public/lib/prism/. The /static/:path* rewrite in vercel.json maps it to the right URL.

Custom shells

If your app uses inherits 'base' with a custom HTML shell that bypasses wrapInDefaultShell, add the tags manually in your base template:

<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/casemaster-prism@1/dist/prism.min.css">
<script type="module" src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/casemaster-prism@1/dist/prism.js"></script>

Per-feature config still works via <script>window.cmsUi = {…}</script> above the prism script tag.

title: Migration guide
lead: Adopt prism on an existing CaseMaster app without breaking layouts. What changes, what to watch for, how to roll back.
body:
Pre-flight checklist

  1. Confirm Bootstrap 4. prism is built for the BS4 markup the runtimes emit. BS5/BS3 not supported yet.
  2. Inventory custom CSS. Anything that uses !important on a Bootstrap class will beat prism's tokens. List those overrides; you'll likely keep them.
  3. Inventory custom JS. Look for jQuery DataTables, custom datepickers, custom row-click handlers. Tag those targets data-cms-no-enhance before installing.
  4. Branding image. If the navbar uses a coloured logo (not white-on-primary), test against --cms-primary. Override the token if needed.
  5. Pick a theme baseline. Default keeps the BS4 feel; Tabler is closest to typical admin UX; Material is the biggest visual departure.

What changes after install

SurfaceBeforeAfter prism
TablesSharp, harsh stripes, blue header.Soft surface, muted-grey uppercase header, hover tint, sortable.
ButtonsBS4 default radii (4 px) and saturated colours.8 px radii, slightly muted hover, focus rings.
InputsBS4 default 4 px radius, blue focus ring.8 px radius, primary-coloured focus ring tied to --cms-primary.
CardsPlain white panel with hairline border.Same panel + soft shadow, rounded corners.
Navbarbg-primary = BS4 blue.bg-primary = your --cms-primary.
ModalsSharp 4 px corners.14 px corners, larger drop shadow, blurred backdrop.
PaginationBS4 attached buttons.Detached buttons with subtle borders.

Common gotchas

RiskMitigation
App ships its own conflicting CSS with !important.We use !important only where Bootstrap forces it. App's !important on a class beats ours on a token. Audit yours, leave them in place.
App already wired jQuery DataTables.Add data-cms-no-enhance on the table or its .dataTables_wrapper.
App uses the runtime's global #cmModal.Modal-link enhancer is opt-in via data-cms-modal-link. We never touch #cmModal.
Print stylesheet looks weird.@media print overrides ship in extras.css. Audit yours after install.
Hard-coded <select> widths break with Tom Select.Set selectThreshold: 99 globally, or tag specific selects data-cms-no-enhance.
Custom sidebar that uses .wms-sidebar with different layout.data-cms-no-enhance on the wrapper disables our sidebar rules.
Right-to-left languages.RTL stylesheet ships; verify against a Hebrew/Arabic test page.

Gradual adoption

You don't have to switch all-or-nothing. Two strategies:

Theme only first

Skip the JS bundle:

/* app.css */
@import url('/static/lib/prism/prism.min.css');
/* don't include the script tag in app.js yet */

You get the visual lift without any behavioural changes. Roll out the JS bundle in a second pass.

Per-page opt-out

Disable prism on a single page that's particularly fragile:

<body data-cms-no-enhance>

The whole page renders bare BS4 even though prism is installed globally.

Roll back

Comment two lines:

/* app.css */
/* @import url('/static/lib/prism/prism.min.css'); */
// app.js
// document.body.appendChild(s);  // (was the prism injection)

Hard-reload. The app reverts to bare Bootstrap 4. No leftover state, no migrations to undo.

title: Troubleshooting
lead: Common problems and their fix paths.
body:
I installed prism but I don't see any visual change

99% of the time this is browser cache. The .NET runtime hardcodes ?v=1.0.0 on app.css / app.js; if you visited the page before installing, your browser keeps serving the old empty content.

Fix: hard-reload (Ctrl+F5). If that doesn't work, DevTools → Network tab → tick "Disable cache" → refresh.

My selects are wrapped twice (Select2 + Tom Select)

Shouldn't happen — prism's forms enhancer checks for .select2-hidden-accessible and skips. If you do see double-wrapping, the runtime probably loaded Select2 after prism, breaking the order.

Fix: ensure app.js (where prism is injected) is the very last script in the page. On the .NET runtime that's the default; on cms-vercel it's controlled by the shell template.

Cmd+K does nothing

Three things to check:

  • Focus trap. If a modal or another widget has captured the keystroke, prism never sees it. Click outside the modal first.
  • Browser shortcut conflict. Some extensions bind Cmd+K. Disable them or rebind via window.cmsUi.shortcuts.openCommand.
  • Disabled in config. window.cmsUi = { command: false }.

Dark mode flickers on page load

The browser paints light then switches when JS sets data-theme="dark". Set the attribute server-side or in an inline <script> in <head> before the body renders.

<script>
  if (localStorage.getItem('theme') === 'dark') {
    document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', 'dark');
  }
</script>

Tables print with grey backgrounds

Most browsers strip background colours by default when printing. If yours doesn't, add to your app CSS:

@media print {
  .table { background: white !important; box-shadow: none !important; }
}

Still stuck?

Open an issue on the GitHub repo with: a minimal page that reproduces the issue, the runtime + version, and the browser. We'll triage.

title: FAQ
body:
Does prism support Bootstrap 5?

Not yet. Both CaseMaster runtimes still ship BS4 markup. If/when one upgrades to BS5, we'll ship a parallel casemaster-prism-bs5 track.

Can I use prism in a React/Vue SPA?

The CSS works anywhere — apply the classes the same way the BS4 markup does. The JS enhancers expect server-rendered DOM and a MutationObserver to catch dynamic inserts. They'll mostly work in an SPA too, but components that mount/unmount frequently may need explicit re-init.

What's the bundle cost?

~14 KB gz CSS (vendor CSS for Tom Select / Flatpickr included), ~10 KB gz core JS. Tom Select (~30 KB gz) and Flatpickr (~20 KB gz) are lazy-loaded only when the first matching element is found.

Does prism need a specific backend?

No. It only touches the rendered HTML. Both CaseMaster runtimes are supported, and the package works on any other BS4-emitting backend with manual <link> + <script> tags.

Do I need to change my .cms source?

No. Variant classes are passed through existing qualifier props (tableClass, class). The default look applies automatically to the runtime-emitted markup.

Can I ship a custom theme?

Yes. Override --cms-* tokens under :root[data-cms-theme="yours"] in your app's CSS. See Building a custom preset.

title: Tables
lead: Sortable headers, filter input, CSV export, column toggle, sticky thead. Auto-detected on every BS4 striped table.
body:
What gets enhanced

Any <table> that matches:

table.cms-table,
table.table.table-striped,
table.table.table-bordered

…and is not opted out via data-cms-no-enhance and has at least minRowsToEnhance rows (default 2).

What we add

  • Toolbar above the table: filter input, Columns dropdown, CSV button, row counter.
  • data-cms-sortable on every header that doesn't carry data-cms-no-sort.
  • data-cms-sort="asc|desc" on the actively sorted column (CSS draws the arrow).
  • data-cms-numeric="1" on detected numeric columns (right-aligned, tabular-nums).
  • Empty-state row when the filter has zero matches.

Opting out

<table class='table table-striped' data-cms-no-enhance>
  <!-- this table won't get the toolbar -->
</table>

<th data-cms-no-sort>Actions</th>
<!-- this column won't be sortable -->

Config

window.cmsUi = {
  tables: {
    search:           true,
    sort:             true,
    csvExport:        true,
    columnToggle:     true,
    minRowsToEnhance: 2,
  },
};

title: Forms
lead: Tom Select for searchable selects, Flatpickr for date inputs. Coexists with Select2.
body:
What gets enhanced

  • <select class="form-control"> with options.length >= selectThreshold (default 8) → Tom Select.
  • <select data-cms-search> regardless of size → Tom Select.
  • <input type="date"> / type="datetime-local" / type="time" → Flatpickr.
  • <input data-cms-date> regardless of type → Flatpickr.

What gets skipped

  • Anything carrying .select2-hidden-accessible (Select2 owns it).
  • Anything carrying .flatpickr-input, .hasDatepicker (jQuery UI), or data-cms-no-enhance.

Lazy loading

Tom Select and Flatpickr modules are loaded as separate chunks (~30 KB gz / ~20 KB gz) only when the first matching element is found. A page with no enhanceable selects or dates downloads zero of this code.

Config

window.cmsUi = {
  forms: {
    tomSelect:       true,
    flatpickr:       true,
    selectThreshold: 8,
  },
};

title: Confirm dialog
lead: Intercept clicks on [data-cms-confirm] and require confirmation before continuing.
body:
Usage

<a href="/delete/42" data-cms-confirm="Delete row 42? This cannot be undone.">
  Delete
</a>

<button form="f"
        data-cms-confirm="Submit changes?"
        data-cms-confirm-title="Please confirm">
  Save
</button>

Behaviour

The first click is intercepted and a styled BS4-modal is rendered with two buttons (Cancel / Confirm). Confirm re-fires the original click; Cancel discards. Escape dismisses.

If the modal can't render for any reason, falls back to the native window.confirm() dialog.

Config

window.cmsUi = { confirm: false }; // disable globally

title: Loading bar
lead: Top-bar progress on link clicks and form submits. Plus busy-button state.
body:
Triggers

  • Click on a same-origin <a> with no target="_blank".
  • Submit on any <form> not tagged data-cms-no-progress.
  • Manual: window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('cms-progress-start')) / 'cms-progress-end'.

Busy buttons

On form submit, the first non-disabled <button type="submit"> gets aria-busy="true". The CSS replaces the button text with a spinner.

Manual API

import { startLoading, endLoading } from 'casemaster-prism';

startLoading();
await fetch('/api/heavy');
endLoading();

title: Toasts
lead: Pop-up status messages from server headers, meta tags, custom events, or direct API.
body:
Triggers

  • Server response header: X-Cms-Toast: {"kind":"success","message":"Saved"} via the cmsFetch helper.
  • Meta tag on initial render (the runtime emits it on POST/redirect): 
    <meta name="cms-toast" content='{"kind":"success","message":"Saved"}'>
  • Custom event from anywhere on the page: 
    window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('cms-toast', {
  detail: { kind: 'warning', title: 'Watch', message: 'Low stock' }
}));
  • Direct API: 
    import { showToast } from 'casemaster-prism';
showToast({ kind: 'success', message: 'Saved' });

Kinds

info · success · warning · danger (alias error).

Auto-dismiss

Toasts auto-dismiss after 5 s by default. Override:

showToast({
  kind: 'info',
  message: 'Sticky',
  autoDismiss: false,   // requires user click
});

showToast({
  kind: 'info',
  message: 'Two seconds',
  durationMs: 2000,
});

title: Keyboard shortcuts
body:
Default shortcuts

ChordAction
/Focus the page's first search input.
?Toggle the shortcut help overlay.
Cmd+K / Ctrl+KOpen command palette.
EscClose active overlay (palette / help).
g dGoto dashboard (configurable).
g iGoto inventory.
g sGoto settings.

Shortcuts are suppressed while the user is typing in a form field.

Override

window.cmsUi = {
  shortcuts: {
    focusSearch:  '/',
    openCommand:  'mod+k',
    toggleHelp:   '?',
    closeOverlay: 'Escape',
    goto: {
      'd': '/page/wms',
      'i': '/page/wms/item',
      's': '/page/settings',
    },
  },
};

title: Modal-link drawer
lead: Open detail pages in a slide-in modal instead of a full page navigation.
body:
Usage

<a href="/inventory/42" data-cms-modal-link>View</a>

The fetched page's <main> / <article> / .cms-content body is extracted and rendered in a right-side drawer. The MutationObserver re-runs other enhancers over the new content so tables/forms inside it work.

Custom title

<a href="/inventory/42"
   data-cms-modal-link
   data-cms-modal-title="Item A-100">View</a>

Coexistence with the .NET runtime's #cmModal

The .NET runtime ships its own iframe-modal as #cmModal. Modal-link doesn't touch it — it's strictly opt-in via data-cms-modal-link. Use the runtime's mechanism for one, prism's for the other; they don't interfere.

title: Inline edit
lead: Click a cell to edit in place. PATCHes back to a configured URL.
body:
Usage

<td data-cms-inline-edit
    data-cms-edit-field="name"
    data-cms-edit-url="/api/inventory/42">
  Widget
</td>

<td data-cms-inline-edit
    data-cms-edit-field="qty"
    data-cms-edit-type="number"
    data-cms-edit-url="/api/inventory/42">
  120
</td>

Affordance

At rest, editable cells carry a dotted underline. On hover, a pencil icon (✎) appears alongside a primary-coloured outline. Click → an inline input replaces the cell content.

Commit / cancel

  • Enter or blur → PATCHes { field: value } to data-cms-edit-url; on success shows a 'Saved' toast.
  • Escape → reverts to the original value.

title: Command palette
lead: Linear-style fuzzy quick switcher. Triggered by <kbd>Cmd</kbd>+<kbd>K</kbd> / <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>K</kbd>.
body:
Item sources

The palette harvests its items from, in priority order:

  1. window.cmsUi.command.items — explicitly provided by the host page.
  2. <a> elements inside the sidebar (.wms-sidebar, .cms-sidebar).
  3. <a> elements inside the navbar (.navbar).

Custom items

window.cmsUi = {
  command: {
    items: [
      { label: 'New sales order', href: '/sales/new', meta: 'Action' },
      { label: 'Run replenishment',
        action: () => fetch('/api/replen', { method: 'POST' }) },
      { label: 'Toggle dark mode',
        action: () => document.documentElement.toggleAttribute('data-theme') },
    ],
  },
};

Each item has label + (href or action) + optional meta (right-aligned label).
